summary.brief: Valerio v. Chaudhry. Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: The plaintiff Kevin Valerio (hereinafter the injured plaintiff), and his wife suing derivatively, commenced this action against the defendants, Asima Chaudhry, Matthew A. T. Clarke, and Central Medical Services of Westbrock, P.C.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: arising from a 'Modified Brostrom Gould Procedure, right foot, repair of anterior talofibular ligament and the calcaneal fibular ligament, right foot' performed on the injured plaintiff on July 8, 2014
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Chaudhry's expert opined that the podiatric and surgical care and treatment provided by Chaudhry to the injured plaintiff did not depart from the accepted standard of podiatric care.
evidence_index.speaker: Chaudhry's expert

evidence_index.text: plaintiffs raised a triable issue of fact by submitting an affirmation of an expert in podiatric surgery, who opined, among other things, that Chaudhry departed from the accepted standard of podiatric and surgical care
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiffs' expert

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, the Supreme Court properly denied those branches of Chaudhry's motion which were for summary judgment dismissing the causes of action alleging medical malpractice and loss of consortium
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Summary judgment is not appropriate in a medical malpractice action where the parties adduce conflicting medical expert opinions
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient underwent a 'Modified Brostrom Gould Procedure' for repair of anterior talofibular ligament and calcaneal fibular ligament on July 8, 2014.

focused_exam.foot: Examination findings not specified in judgment

requestables.name: Podiatric Surgery Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with a podiatric surgeon regarding the procedure and postoperative care

reveal.result_summary: Consultation performed but details not documented in judgment

decision_points.clinical_context: During the Modified Brostrom Gould Procedure
decision_points.prompt: What intraoperative measures should be taken to prevent complications?

options.description: Inspect talar dome for loose particles and ensure proper DVT prophylaxis
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care to prevent postoperative complications

options.description: Proceed without inspecting talar dome or ensuring DVT prophylaxis
options.clinical_reasoning: Neglecting these measures increases risk of complications

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not inspect talar dome or ensure DVT prophylaxis

expected_action_court.description: Inspection and DVT prophylaxis should have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established these as standard care

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to perform standard intraoperative measures

key_findings: Conflicting expert opinions precluded summary judgment
Issues of fact regarding standard of care and causation

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper measures could have prevented complications

allegations.allegation: Failure to perform standard intraoperative measures

court_findings.finding: Conflicting expert opinions precluded summary judgment
court_findings.legal_significance: Issues of fact regarding standard of care and causation
